heading: Additional jurisdiction in certain trafficking offenses
label: 1596 Additional jurisdiction in certain trafficking offenses

children.text: an alleged offender is a national of the United States or an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence (as those terms are defined in section 101 of the Immigration and Nationality Act ()); or8 U.S.C. 1101
an alleged offender is present in the United States, irrespective of the nationality of the alleged offender.

children.heading: .—Limitation on Prosecutions of Offenses Prosecuted in Other Countries
children.label: b .—Limitation on Prosecutions of Offenses Prosecuted in Other Countries
children.text: No prosecution may be commenced against a person under this section if a foreign government, in accordance with jurisdiction recognized by the United States, has prosecuted or is prosecuting such person for the conduct constituting such offense, except upon the approval of the Attorney General or the Deputy Attorney General (or a person acting in either such capacity), which function of approval may not be delegated.
